forecasts.reasoning: Given the March 2025 report of Discord partnering with JP Morgan and Goldman Sachs for a 2025 IPO, a timeline of fewer than 4 months (before July 1) to reaching a public S-1 filing or a formal IPO announcement is highly accelerated. Companies typically spend 2-4 months drafting the S-1, followed by a confidential submission and a 30-day SEC review period before any public filing. Targeting a standard late Q3 (September) or Q4 IPO would place the public S-1 filing in August or late July, which falls after the July 1 deadline. Furthermore, SEC gun-jumping rules restrict casual announcements on company blogs, meaning the resolution relies almost entirely on an accelerated S-1 filing or a formal press release about a confidential submission. Considering the base rate for such timelines, widespread delays in IPOs, and typical regulatory constraints, the probability of meeting the before-July-1 threshold is relatively low.
